mnemonics.title: FRIEND Key Points
mnemonics.content: FRIEND - F-R-I-E-N-D: F-unction not member, R-ules not transitive/inherited, I-ndex anywhere in class, E-xternal function, N-o this pointer, D-ata access directly

acronyms.expansion: Function that is not a member but has special access to private data
acronyms.usage: Used to remember that friend functions are non-members with special privileges

visualTips.visualization: Think of the class as a house with walls (private). Friend function is like a special guest pass written on the door that allows entry to specific non-member visitors.
Visualize two buildings connected by a bridge - the friend class can walk across the bridge to access the other's private rooms freely.
Draw three circles A, B, C where arrows show friendship. Note that arrows don't transfer - A→B and B→C doesn't create A→C.

analogies.analogy: Like giving a house key to a specific trusted friend - they aren't family members (not part of the class) but have access to your private rooms (private data).
Like hiring a property management company - the entire company (friend class) gets access to all your property's private areas.
If your friend knows your spouse, it doesn't mean your friend automatically knows all your spouse's friends.
